**ACL-service method values and factory closures (all over `s`):**
| ① Field | ④ Bound at |
|---|---|
| `agent/grpc-external/services/acl/server.go:23` `LoadAuthMethod` | `agent/consul/server_grpc.go:363-365` — closure calling `s.loadAuthMethod` |
| `agent/grpc-external/services/acl/server.go:24` `NewLogin` | `agent/consul/server_grpc.go:368` — `func() aclgrpc.Login { return s.aclLogin() }` |
| `agent/grpc-external/services/acl/server.go:26` `ValidateEnterpriseRequest` | `agent/consul/server_grpc.go:371` — method value `s.validateEnterpriseRequest` |
| `agent/grpc-external/services/acl/server.go:27` `LocalTokensEnabled` | `agent/consul/server_grpc.go:366` — method value `s.LocalTokensEnabled` |
| `agent/grpc-external/services/acl/server.go:30` `NewTokenWriter` | `agent/consul/server_grpc.go:369` — `func() aclgrpc.TokenWriter { return s.aclTokenWriter() }` |

**Back-edge out of the server into `Deps`-owned objects** (same mechanism, opposite direction):
- `agent/consul/server.go:579` — `s.connPool.GatewayResolver = s.gatewayLocator.PickGateway`
- `agent/consul/server.go:580` — `s.grpcConnPool.SetGatewayResolver(s.gatewayLocator.PickGateway)`

Both store a **method value bound to `s.gatewayLocator`**, which holds `s` (Carrier L). The pools outlive the server, so this is a retention *escape* from the server's lifetime.

---

## Carrier Q — `*Agent` (`&a`), reaching the server through `a.delegate`
③ `agent/agent.go:234`; `Agent.RPC` at `agent/agent.go:1681` forwards to `a.delegate` (bound at `:709`).

| ① Field | ② Interface | ④ Bound at |
|---|---|---|
| `agent/setup.go:77` `LazyNetRPC.rpc NetRPC` | `agent/setup.go:71-73` (1 method) | `agent/agent.go:531` — `bd.NetRPC.SetNetRPC(&a)`, written under `r.mu` at `setup.go:80-84`, read under `RLock` at `:86-89`. **The only mutex-protected route in the whole audit** |

---

## Carrier R — `ServerDataSourceDeps` (a struct value carrying two server handles)
③ `agent/proxycfg-glue/glue.go:29-36`. Built once at `agent/agent.go:4653-4660` with `ACLResolver: a.delegate` (`:4658`, the server via Carrier L) and `GetStore:` closure (`:4659`, Carrier P). Guarded by `if server != nil` at `agent/agent.go:4652`. It is **copied by value** into 20 data sources; each copy is an independent retention.

| ① Field (all typed `deps ServerDataSourceDeps`) | ④ Bound at |
|---|---|
| `agent/proxycfg-glue/config_entry.go:44` | `agent/agent.go:4661` (`ServerConfigEntry`) / `:4662` (`ServerConfigEntryList`) |
| `agent/proxycfg-glue/config_entry.go:99` | `agent/proxycfg-glue/config_entry.go:55` — `newConfigEntryRequest(req, deps)` |
| `agent/proxycfg-glue/discovery_chain.go:37` | `agent/agent.go:4663` |
| `agent/proxycfg-glue/exported_peered_services.go:33` | `agent/agent.go:4664` |
| `agent/proxycfg-glue/federation_state_list_mesh_gateways.go:33` | `agent/agent.go:4665` |
| `agent/proxycfg-glue/gateway_services.go:33` | `agent/agent.go:4666` |
| `agent/proxycfg-glue/health.go:45` | `agent/proxycfg-glue/health.go:40` (`ServerHealth`) — currently **not wired**; call site commented out at `agent/agent.go:4670` |
| `agent/proxycfg-glue/health.go:63` | `agent/proxycfg-glue/health.go:74` — into the `LocalMaterializerDeps` |
| `agent/proxycfg-glue/health_blocking.go:46` | `agent/agent.go:4671` |
| `agent/proxycfg-glue/service_http_checks.go:30` | `agent/agent.go:4672` |
| `agent/proxycfg-glue/intentions.go:75` | `agent/agent.go:4673` |
| `agent/proxycfg-glue/intention_upstreams.go:49` | `agent/agent.go:4674` and `:4675` (**two** instances share the struct) |
| `agent/proxycfg-glue/internal_service_dump.go:56` | `agent/agent.go:4676` |
| `agent/proxycfg-glue/peering_list.go:33` | `agent/agent.go:4677` |
| `agent/proxycfg-glue/peered_upstreams.go:32` | `agent/agent.go:4678` |
| `agent/proxycfg-glue/resolved_service_config.go:34` | `agent/agent.go:4679` |
| `agent/proxycfg-glue/service_list.go:31` | `agent/agent.go:4680` |
| `agent/proxycfg-glue/service_list.go:48` | `agent/proxycfg-glue/service_list.go:64` — `serviceListRequest.deps`, per materializer |
| `agent/proxycfg-glue/trust_bundle.go:35` | `agent/agent.go:4681` |
| `agent/proxycfg-glue/trust_bundle.go:83` | `agent/agent.go:4682` |

---

## What the traces reveal about the rework

1. **Only two routes are re-bindable after construction:** `rate.Handler.Register` (`agent/consul/rate/handler.go:337`) and `LazyNetRPC.SetNetRPC` (`agent/setup.go:80`). Of those, only the latter is mutex-protected — the former races if you re-register a live handler.

2. **Two binding sites dominate**: `agent/consul/server_grpc.go:181-542` (35 routes) and `agent/agent.go:4653-4682` (20 routes). Fix the lifecycle at those two sites and 55 of 74 routes follow.

3. **Closures are the blind spot.** ~25 routes bind the server through a `func` field. No holder file imports `agent/consul` or names `*Server`; the only evidence is at the binding site. Any audit tool that scans holder structs will report these as clean.

4. **Narrowing compounds.** `PeeringBackend` is re-narrowed three times (11-method `Backend` → 1-method `SubscriptionBackend` → 1-method `Subscriber`) at `server_grpc.go:387` → `stream_resources.go:350` → `subscription_manager.go:750`, the last one *per exported service per peer stream*. There is no upper bound on how many live `Subscriber` handles exist at a given moment.

5. **Three holders retain something whose lifetime does not match the server's**: `*stream.EventPublisher` (agent-owned, server-stopped — Carrier N), `*ACLResolver` (server-owned, separately closed at `server.go:1297` — Carrier M), and `servercert.CertManager.getStore` (captured in the agent, cancelled by the *agent's* `shutdownCh` at `agent/agent.go:723`). Each is a place where "replace the server" and "replace what the server holds" must be decided independently.

content.text:
# Blast radius: server lifecycle rework

**Scope:** 106 retaining fields (Task 4), 74 of them behind a non-server type (Task 5). Two binding sites dominate: `agent/consul/server_grpc.go:181-542` and `agent/agent.go:4653-4682`.

---

## The framing that decides everything

**A lifecycle change alone produces zero compiler errors — anywhere.**

If the rework keeps the type `*consul.Server` and only changes *when* it is created/destroyed/replaced, then all 106 holders compile unchanged, including the 32 that name `*Server` directly. The compiler only helps if you **change the type** (e.g. `*Server` → `ServerHandle`), and even then it helps at exactly 32 of 106 sites (Task 4 §1). The other 74 are typed as locally-declared interfaces or `func` values that a new server type would still satisfy — **74 silent sites**.

Any "replace the server" flow that calls `Shutdown()` **kills the agent's shared connection pool**, which the replacement server then inherits dead at `server.go:502`. Nothing in `NewServer` re-creates it. This breaks before any holder is even considered.

Compounding back-edge: `agent/consul/server.go:579-580` writes `s.connPool.GatewayResolver = s.gatewayLocator.PickGateway` and `s.grpcConnPool.SetGatewayResolver(...)` — a method value bound to the *old* `GatewayLocator`, which holds the *old* server (`agent/consul/gateway_locator.go:46`). ## Re-verification checklist

**Shutdown must become joinable.** Today `Shutdown` (`agent/consul/server.go:1241-1306`) closes one channel (`:1251`) and returns. No WaitGroup, no join. The ~16 goroutines started in `NewServer` (`:531`, `:570`, `:670`, `:728`, `:752`, `:781`, `:784`, `:787`, `:788`, `:822`, `:824`, `:832`, `:848`, `:852`, `:856`, `:860`) are still running when it returns. Replacement without a join means old and new servers run concurrently against the same raft data dir, serf ports, and gRPC handler tables.

Ordered checks:
1. `agent/consul/server.go:1293` — stop shutting down the agent-owned conn pool.
2. `agent/consul/server.go:579-580` — re-point both gateway-resolver back-edges.
3. `agent/consul/server.go:791`, `:796`, `:819`, `:871` — add the missing `s.Shutdown()` on these four error returns.
4. `agent/consul/server.go:1255` vs `:435` — `routineManager` is never stopped, only `leaderRoutineManager`. Fix before any restart loop.
5. `agent/consul/rate/handler.go:337` — add a mutex; then decide the double-register at `server.go:525` + `agent/agent.go:710`.
6. `agent/agent.go:719`/`:723` — servercert on the agent's channel.
7. `agent/consul/rpc.go:128` — `if s.shutdown` read without holding `shutdownLock` (written at `server.go:1250` under the lock). Already a race; a restart makes it observable.
8. `agent/consul/server.go:570` and `:568` — publisher and rate-limiter `Run` contexts tied to the server's channel for agent-owned objects.
9. `agent/agent.go:4652` — the `if server != nil` snapshot; 20 copies downstream.
10. Every `//go:generate mockery` interface listed above — write at least one test that exercises the **real** wiring, since the mocks guarantee the existing suite cannot fail.

**Highest-value structural change:** give the ~74 non-typed routes something to re-wire *through*. The `LazyNetRPC` pattern (`agent/setup.go:75-95`) — a small mutex-guarded indirection object, injected once, re-pointed on replacement — already exists in this codebase and would collapse P0 items 2, 3, and 5 into a single seam.

Each service is built, registered, and the local variable dropped: `srv.Register(reg)` then `return nil` at `server_grpc.go:521` (serverdiscovery), `:492` (connectca), `:507` (dataplane), `:404` (peerstream), `:438` (peering), `:459` (operator), `:539` (configentry), `:376` (acl), `:352` (resource), `:471` (subscribe). **The only reference is the gRPC handler table.** There is no handle to re-wire *through*, even if you wanted to.

The retained fields, all with no compile-time tie:
- **`ForwardRPC` closures over `s` and `s.grpcConnPool`** (6): `acl/server.go:25` ← `server_grpc.go:359`; `configentry/server.go:37` ← `:531`; `connectca/server.go:33` ← `:484`; `peerstream/server.go:40` ← `:393`; `agent/rpc/peering/service.go:92` ← `:422`; `agent/rpc/operator/service.go:73` ← `:447`.
- **`GetStore` closures over `s.FSM().State()`** (4): `connectca/server.go:29` ← `:480`; `dataplane/server.go:24` ← `:499`; `peerstream/server.go:38` ← `:388`; plus `peerstream/subscription_manager.go:52` ← `stream_resources.go:351`.
- **ACL-service method values / factories over `s`** (5): `acl/server.go:23` ← `:363`; `:24` ← `:368`; `:26` ← `:371`; `:27` ← `:366`; `:30` ← `:369`.
- **Direct `FSMServer: s`** (2): `configentry/server.go:38` ← `:534`; `agent/rpc/peering/service.go:99` ← `:433`. These *would* catch a type change (assertion at `agent/consul/server.go:165`), but not a lifetime change.

**Re-verify:** the external gRPC server (`agent/consul/server.go:279`) is agent-owned and **not** stopped by `Shutdown` — so every service registered on it at `server_grpc.go:293`, `:317`, `:268`, `:190` outlives the server by construction and keeps serving requests through dead closures.

### 4. `servercert.CertManager` — cancelled by the wrong channel
`agent/consul/servercert/manager.go:78` `getStore func() Store` ← closure over `consulServer.FSM().State()` at **`agent/agent.go:719`**, started at **`agent/agent.go:723`** against `&lib.StopChannelContext{StopCh: a.shutdownCh}` — **the agent's channel, not the server's**.

This is a guaranteed leak: server shutdown closes `s.shutdownCh` (`server.go:1251`) and abandons the state store (`server.go:1302`), but this goroutine keeps running and calling `getStore()` on the abandoned `*state.Store`. It also holds `a.leafCertManager` and writes into `a.tlsConfigurator` (`manager.go:81`), so a stale instance can push certs derived from the dead server's CA config.

**Re-verify:** channel choice at `agent/agent.go:723`; whether `certManager` needs a stop/restart path alongside the server.

### 5. `rate.Handler.serversStatusProvider` — race on the one existing seam
`agent/consul/rate/handler.go:168`, iface at `:195-202`, set by the setter at `:337-339` — **a plain unsynchronised field write**:

```go
func (h *Handler) Register(serversStatusProvider ServersStatusProvider) {
	h.serversStatusProvider = serversStatusProvider
}
```

Called twice today: `agent/consul/server.go:525` (mid-construction, before the server is usable) and `agent/agent.go:710`. Read on the hot path at `agent/consul/rate/handler.go:310` inside every rate-limit decision, from many goroutines. The `Handler` is agent-owned and outlives the server; the server also retains it (`agent/consul/server.go:323`) and drives it (`:568` `Run(shutdownCh)`) — **mutual retention across the lifetime boundary**, so the limiter's own goroutine dies with the first server.

Re-registering on replacement is a data race today. It's the closest thing to a working seam and it needs a mutex before it can be one.

**Re-verify:** add synchronisation at `handler.go:337`; decide whether `Run`'s context should come from the agent instead of `server.go:568`.

---

## P1 — Likely to leak, unbounded or lifetime-inverted

### 6. peerstream: unbounded per-stream handles
`agent/grpc-external/services/peerstream/subscription_manager.go:48` (`config Config`), `:51` (`backend SubscriptionBackend`), `:52` (`getStore`) — created **per peer stream** at `stream_resources.go:345-353`; and `subscription_view.go:28` (`sub Subscriber`) — created **per exported service per stream** at `subscription_manager.go:750`.

`PeeringBackend` (`agent/consul/peering_backend.go:33`) is re-narrowed three times: 11-method `Backend` (`peerstream/server.go:90-112`) → 1-method `SubscriptionBackend` (`subscription_manager.go:41`) → 1-method `Subscriber` (`subscription_view.go:21`). By the third hop nothing resembles a server. There is **no bound** on how many live copies exist, and peer streams are long-lived by design, so a restart strands an arbitrary number of them.

`PeeringBackend` is also **mutated from the leadership loop**: `SetLeaderAddress` (`peering_backend.go:51`) called from `trackLeaderChanges()` (`agent/consul/server.go:824`). A stranded backend keeps a frozen `leaderAddr` (`peering_backend.go:36`) and hands stale dial addresses to peers.

**Re-verify:** stream teardown on server replacement; whether `Tracker` (`server_grpc.go:420`) survives.

### 7. `*stream.EventPublisher` — agent-owned, server-killed
Created by the agent (`BaseDeps`), retained at `agent/consul/server.go:418`, and **run by the server**: `go s.publisher.Run(&lib.StopChannelContext{StopCh: s.shutdownCh})` at `agent/consul/server.go:570`. Holders that extracted it: `connectca/server.go:28` ← `server_grpc.go:479`; `serverdiscovery/server.go:22` ← `server_grpc.go:514`; `agent/submatview/local_materializer.go:32` ← `proxycfg-glue/service_list.go:66`; plus `PeeringBackend.Subscribe` reaching it via `s.srv.publisher` (`peering_backend.go:287`).

Server shutdown stops a publisher the agent still owns and other subsystems still hold. Restarting calls `Run` on an already-stopped publisher.

### 8. `*ACLResolver` — closed by the server, held by six subsystems
`agent/consul/server.go:180` (embedded), created `:640`, **closed at `server.go:1297-1299`**. Extracted into: `connectca/server.go:31` ← `server_grpc.go:482`; `dataplane/server.go:26` ← `:501`; `peerstream/server.go:41` ← `:390`; `serverdiscovery/server.go:23` ← `:515`/`:291`; `resource/server_ce.go:36` ← `:200`; `agent/submatview/local_materializer.go:33` ← `proxycfg-glue` deps (`agent/agent.go:4658`, where it's actually `a.delegate`).

After `Close()`, these six authorize against a closed cache. Note the divergence: five hold `*ACLResolver`, the submatview one holds the **server**. Both need answers, and they're different answers.

### 9. Controller runtime — `internal/multicluster`
`internal/multicluster/internal/controllers/register.go:12` and `.../v1compat/controller.go:82` hold `v1ServiceExportsShim{s}` (`agent/consul/v2_config_entry_exports_shim.go:24`), bound at `agent/consul/server.go:879-880`. The reconciler is retained by `s.controllerManager` (`server.go:438`), started `go s.controllerManager.Run(shutdownCh)` at `:822`.

`registerControllers` returns an error path at `agent/consul/server.go:819-821` that **does not call `s.Shutdown()`** — same for `:791`, `:796`, `:871`. Those four returns leak every goroutine started up to that point. Any restart-on-failure loop hits this immediately.

### 10. Autopilot library retention
`agent/consul/autopilot.go:36` `server *Server` inside `AutopilotDelegate`, built at `autopilot.go:81-87`, handed to `autopilot.New(s.raft, apDelegate, …)` at `autopilot.go:89-91`. The **third-party library** retains it; `s.autopilot.Start(shutdownCh)` at `agent/consul/server.go:865`, and `Leave()` calls `s.autopilot.NumVoters()` at `server.go:1334`. Also embeds `ReadyServersEventPublisher` with its own `GetStore` closure at `autopilot.go:85`. No stop call in `Shutdown` — only the channel.

### 11. `internal/storage/raft` — handle bound before raft exists
`internal/storage/raft/backend.go:81` `handle Handle` ← `raftstorage.NewBackend(&raftHandle{s}, …)` at `agent/consul/server.go:527`, and `internal/storage/raft/forwarding.go:116` ← `forwarding.go:123`. Bound at line 527, but `setupRaft()` doesn't run until `server.go:659` — the handle is live for 130 lines of construction while `s.raft` is nil. `go s.raftStorageBackend.Run(shutdownCh)` starts at `:531`, i.e. **before raft exists**. Any construction reordering or retry must preserve this or panic.

### 12. `CAManager` → `ConsulProvider` — two indirections, re-created lazily
`agent/consul/leader_connect_ca.go:61` `delegate caServerDelegate` ← `caDelegateWithState{Server: s}` at `agent/consul/server.go:664`; then `agent/connect/ca/provider_consul.go:35` `Delegate` ← `ca.NewConsulProvider(c.delegate, logger)` at `leader_connect_ca.go:462`. Because providers are re-created on CA re-init, a stale `CAManager.delegate` **propagates into freshly-created providers** — the bug survives the thing that looks like it would clear it. `CAManager` is also leader-scoped (`leaderRoutineManager`, `server.go:1255`), so it has a second lifecycle interacting with the first.

---

## P2 — Real risk, but a seam or a guard exists

### 13. `agent.Agent.delegate` — `agent/agent.go:249`
Bound at `agent/agent.go:709`. A plain field, no lock, read from ~66 call sites (`Agent` is the #5 hub symbol per `sense_status`). It *is* re-assignable, which makes it the natural seam — but every reader assumes it never changes. `agent/agent.go:1788` guards `if a.delegate != nil`; nothing guards "is this the current one".

### 14. `LazyNetRPC.rpc` — `agent/setup.go:77`
The **best-behaved route in the codebase**: setter at `agent/setup.go:80-84` under `r.mu`, read under `RLock` at `:86-89`, explicit not-ready error at `:92`. Bound at `agent/agent.go:531` via `bd.NetRPC.SetNetRPC(&a)`. Use this as the template for the other 105.

### 15. `local.State.Delegate` — `agent/local/state.go:179`
Bound at `agent/agent.go:762`. Settable field, no lock, but the surrounding `State` has a `sync.RWMutex` at `state.go:173` that does **not** cover `Delegate`. The comment at `state.go:177` already documents the ordering hazard. Anti-entropy runs continuously against it.

### 16. In-package interface holders with no seam
`agent/consul/gateway_locator.go:46` (`serverDelegate`, iface `:276`) ← `server.go:573`, driven by `go …Run(shutdownCh)` at `:670`; `agent/consul/reporting/reporting.go:21` (`ServerDelegate`, iface `:53`) ← `server.go:786`, two goroutines at `:787-788`; `agent/consul/replication.go:247` (`IndexReplicatorDelegate`, iface `:221`) ← `server.go:608`; `agent/consul/auto_config_endpoint.go:159` (`AutoConfigBackend`, iface `:143`) ← `server.go:1203`; `agent/consul/acl.go:282` and `:170` (`ACLResolverBackend`, iface `:139`) ← `server.go:633`/`:640`.

### 17. Wrapper-carrier routes (single indirection, greppable)
`agent/grpc-internal/services/subscribe/subscribe.go:24` ← `subscribeBackend` (`agent/consul/subscribe_backend.go:16`) ← `server_grpc.go:466`; `agent/grpc-external/services/configentry/server.go:35` ← `ConfigEntryBackend` (`configentry_backend.go:13`) ← `server_grpc.go:529`; `agent/rpc/operator/service.go:71` ← `OperatorBackend` (`operator_backend.go:17`) ← `server_grpc.go:445`; `agent/grpc-external/services/resource/server_ce.go:39` ← `V1TenancyBridge` (`tenancy_bridge.go:12`) ← `server_grpc.go:344`; `agent/grpc-external/services/connectca/server.go:32` ← `s.caManager` ← `server_grpc.go:483`; `agent/grpc-external/services/resource/server_ce.go:35` ← `s.storageBackend` ← `server_grpc.go:186`/`:200`.

### 18. `xdscapacity.Controller` — `agent/consul/xdscapacity/capacity.go:47`
`cfg.GetStore` closure over `s.fsm.State()` at `agent/consul/server.go:828`; `go …Run(shutdownCh)` at `:832`. Also holds `flat.XDSStreamLimiter` (`:829`), an agent-owned object — another cross-boundary retention.

---

## P3 — Compile-time visible, mechanical
The 32 direct `*Server` fields from Task 4 §1. A *type* change flags all of them; a pure lifetime change flags none. Mechanically the easiest to fix, and the 26 registered RPC endpoints (`agent/consul/server.go:1176`, from the package-global `endpoints` slice at `:1162`) are uniform — one loop rebuilds them all. The awkward ones are the two embedders, `agent/consul/acl_server.go:110-113` and `agent/consul/leader_connect_ca.go:90-92`, where method promotion means the wrapper's API changes if the server's does; both already carry `// TODO: un-embed` / narrowing TODOs.

---

## Confirmed NOT at risk — don't spend effort here
Verified against construction sites; these hold `*fsm.FSM` or `*state.Store`, which are genuine cut points:
- `agent/consul/server_overview.go:22` `stateProvider` ← `NewOverviewManager(s.logger, s.fsm, …)` (`server.go:783`)
- `agent/consul/usagemetrics/usagemetrics.go:133` and `:77` ← `WithStateProvider(s.fsm)` (`server.go:763`); doc at `usagemetrics.go:122` confirms
- `agent/consul/reporting/reporting.go:22` `stateProvider StateDelegate` ← `s.fsm.State()` (`server.go:786`) — but the sibling field at `:21` **is** a P1 holder
- `consul.checkServersProvider` (`agent/consul/util.go:71`) — parameter-only at `util.go:126`, `:137`; never stored

---

## Re-verification checklist

**Shutdown must become joinable.** Today `Shutdown` (`agent/consul/server.go:1241-1306`) closes one channel (`:1251`) and returns. No WaitGroup, no join. The ~16 goroutines started in `NewServer` (`:531`, `:570`, `:670`, `:728`, `:752`, `:781`, `:784`, `:787`, `:788`, `:822`, `:824`, `:832`, `:848`, `:852`, `:856`, `:860`) are still running when it returns. Replacement without a join means old and new servers run concurrently against the same raft data dir, serf ports, and gRPC handler tables.

Ordered checks:
1. `agent/consul/server.go:1293` — stop shutting down the agent-owned conn pool.
2. `agent/consul/server.go:579-580` — re-point both gateway-resolver back-edges.
3. `agent/consul/server.go:791`, `:796`, `:819`, `:871` — add the missing `s.Shutdown()` on these four error returns.
4. `agent/consul/server.go:1255` vs `:435` — `routineManager` is never stopped, only `leaderRoutineManager`. Fix before any restart loop.
5. `agent/consul/rate/handler.go:337` — add a mutex; then decide the double-register at `server.go:525` + `agent/agent.go:710`.
6. `agent/agent.go:719`/`:723` — servercert on the agent's channel.
7. `agent/consul/rpc.go:128` — `if s.shutdown` read without holding `shutdownLock` (written at `server.go:1250` under the lock). Already a race; a restart makes it observable.
8. `agent/consul/server.go:570` and `:568` — publisher and rate-limiter `Run` contexts tied to the server's channel for agent-owned objects.
9. `agent/agent.go:4652` — the `if server != nil` snapshot; 20 copies downstream.
10. Every `//go:generate mockery` interface listed above — write at least one test that exercises the **real** wiring, since the mocks guarantee the existing suite cannot fail.

**Highest-value structural change:** give the ~74 non-typed routes something to re-wire *through*. The `LazyNetRPC` pattern (`agent/setup.go:75-95`) — a small mutex-guarded indirection object, injected once, re-pointed on replacement — already exists in this codebase and would collapse P0 items 2, 3, and 5 into a single seam.

# Landing map: server lifecycle rework

Everything below is verified against the tree at `HEAD` (`2f37d52`). 106 retaining fields, 74 with no compile-time tie. Land in the 5 phases in §F.

---

## A. The seam to introduce

The codebase already contains the right pattern, in production, working: **`LazyNetRPC`** at `agent/setup.go:75-95` — a mutex-guarded indirection, injected once at `agent/agent.go:531`, re-pointable via `SetNetRPC` (`agent/setup.go:80-84`), with a not-ready error at `:92`.

Generalise it. Introduce one indirection object (`ServerHandle`) created by the **agent**, injected into every subsystem in place of `*Server` / narrowed views / closures over `s`, and re-pointed on replacement. Without it, 104 of 106 routes have nowhere to write the new pointer.

Two properties are non-negotiable, both learned from failures in the current code:
- **Mutex-guarded** — `rate.Handler.Register` (`agent/consul/rate/handler.go:337-339`) is the counter-example: an unsynchronised write read on the hot path at `handler.go:310`.
- **Explicit not-ready state** — `NewServer` distributes `s` from `agent/consul/server.go:525` onward while `s.fsm`, `s.raft`, `s.ACLResolver` are all still nil (created at `:535`, `:659`, `:640`).

---

## B. Server-side code to edit

### B1. `agent/consul/server.go` — construction
| Line | Edit |
|---|---|
| `:169-444` | `Server` struct. Split ownership: fields sourced from `Deps` (`connPool` `:216`, `grpcConnPool` `:219`, `publisher` `:418`, `tokens` `:213`, `router` `:308`, `tlsConfigurator` `:337`, `externalGRPCServer` `:279`, `incomingRPCLimiter` `:323`, `registry` `:443`) are **borrowed, not owned** — mark them and stop destroying them in `Shutdown` |
| `:398-400` | `shutdown` / `shutdownCh` / `shutdownLock`. Add a `sync.WaitGroup` (or `errgroup`) so `Shutdown` can join |
| `:467-876` | `NewServer`. The linear body must become restart-safe |
| `:499-524` | Struct literal — all channels created here, never recreated |
| `:525` | `incomingRPCLimiter.Register(s)` — **first escape of a half-built `s`**; move after construction completes |
| `:527` | `raftHandle{s}` bound 132 lines before `setupRaft()` at `:659` |
| `:531` | `go s.raftStorageBackend.Run(...)` starts **before raft exists** — preserve or fix explicitly |
| `:568`, `:570` | `incomingRPCLimiter.Run(shutdownCh)` and `go s.publisher.Run(shutdownCh)` — server-scoped contexts on **agent-owned** objects. Move to agent-scoped |
| `:573-580` | `NewGatewayLocator(…, s, …)` **and** the two back-edges `s.connPool.GatewayResolver = s.gatewayLocator.PickGateway` / `s.grpcConnPool.SetGatewayResolver(...)` — must be re-pointed or unset on replacement |
| `:608-612` | `IndexReplicator{Delegate: &FederationStateReplicator{srv: s, …}}` |
| `:633` | `&serverACLResolverBackend{Server: s}` |
| `:664` | `NewCAManager(&caDelegateWithState{Server: s}, …)` |
| `:763`, `:783`, `:786`, `:828` | `WithStateProvider(s.fsm)` / `NewOverviewManager(…, s.fsm, …)` / `NewReportingManager(…, s, s.fsm.State(), …)` / `xdscapacity GetStore` closure |
| `:791`, `:796`, `:819`, `:871` | **Four error returns that do not call `s.Shutdown()`** — leak every goroutine started so far. Fix first; a restart loop hits these immediately |
| `:822`, `:824`, `:832`, `:848`, `:852`, `:856`, `:860`, `:865`, `:868` | Remaining goroutine starts — all must register with the WaitGroup |
| `:879-880` | `NewExportedServicesShim(s)` → `RegisterCompatControllers` |
| `:1162`, `:1165`, `:1176` | Package-global `endpoints` slice + `registerEndpoint` + `s.rpcServer.Register(fn(s))` — the one loop that rebuilds all 26 RPC endpoints |
| `:1203` | `NewAutoConfig(…, autoConfigBackend{Server: s}, …)` |

### B2. `agent/consul/server.go:1241-1306` — `Shutdown`
| Line | Edit |
|---|---|
| `:1246-1251` | Idempotency guard + `close(shutdownCh)`. Add WaitGroup join **after** the closes |
| `:1255` | `leaderRoutineManager.StopAll()` — but `routineManager` (`:435`) is **never stopped**. Add it |
| `:1268` | `// TODO: actually shutdown areas?` — resolve; stale router areas break rejoin |
| `:1286-1290` | `internalGRPCHandler.Shutdown()` — note `externalGRPCServer` (`:279`) is *not* stopped here, so 4 services registered on it (`server_grpc.go:190`, `:268`, `:293`, `:317`) outlive the server |
| **`:1293`** | **`s.connPool.Shutdown()` — destroys an agent-owned pool created at `agent/setup.go:165`. Remove or move to the agent.** Highest-priority single line |
| `:1297-1299` | `s.ACLResolver.Close()` — six subsystems hold this resolver (§C4) |
| `:1301-1303` | `s.fsm.State().Abandon()` — the invalidation signal every `GetStore` closure depends on |

### B3. `agent/consul/server_grpc.go:181-542` — 35 routes, one file
Every `register*Server` builds a service, calls `srv.Register(reg)`, and **drops the handle** (`:352`, `:376`, `:404`, `:438`, `:459`, `:471`, `:492`, `:507`, `:521`, `:539`). Each must take the indirection object instead of capturing `s`:
`:344` `NewV1TenancyBridge(s)` · `:347` resource config · `:359-371` six ACL closures/method values · `:387-399` peerstream · `:419-433` peering (incl. `FSMServer: s` at `:433`) · `:445-453` operator · `:466` subscribeBackend · `:479-487` connectca · `:499-502` dataplane · `:514-516` serverdiscovery · `:529-534` configentry (incl. `FSMServer: s` at `:534`) · `:229` `s.peeringBackend = NewPeeringBackend(s)`.

### B4. `agent/agent.go` — process layer
| Line | Edit |
|---|---|
| `:153-213` | `delegate` interface — the ~15-method narrowed handle |
| `:249` | `delegate delegate` field — no lock; ~66 readers |
| `:531` | `bd.NetRPC.SetNetRPC(&a)` — the template |
| `:704` | `consul.NewServer(...)` — sole production construction site |
| `:709` | `a.delegate = consulServer` |
| `:710` | `incomingRPCLimiter.Register(consulServer)` — duplicate of `server.go:525` |
| `:719`, `:723` | servercert `GetStore` closure + `Start(a.shutdownCh)` — **wrong channel**, guaranteed leak |
| `:762` | `a.State.Delegate = a.delegate` |
| `:1713`, `:1811` | `ShutdownAgent` / `ShutdownEndpoints` — where a replace/restart path would live |
| `:4652-4682` | `if server != nil` guard + `ServerDataSourceDeps` → 20 copies |

### B5. `agent/consul/rate/handler.go`
`:168` field · `:195-202` interface · `:310` hot-path read · **`:337-339` add a mutex to `Register`**.

### B6. `agent/consul/rpc.go:128`
`if s.shutdown` read without `shutdownLock` (written at `server.go:1250` under the lock). Already a race; a restart makes it observable.

---

## C. Holders to review, by area and route

**74 non-typed + 32 typed = 106.** Every route's carrier and binding site from Task 5.

### C1. Discarded gRPC holders — closures over `s` (25 routes) · **no handle exists to re-wire**
`ForwardRPC` (6): `acl/server.go:25`←`server_grpc.go:359` · `configentry/server.go:37`←`:531` · `connectca/server.go:33`←`:484` · `peerstream/server.go:40`←`:393` · `agent/rpc/peering/service.go:92`←`:422` · `agent/rpc/operator/service.go:73`←`:447`

`config_entry.go:44`←`agent.go:4661`/`:4662` · `config_entry.go:99`←`config_entry.go:55` · `discovery_chain.go:37`←`:4663` · `exported_peered_services.go:33`←`:4664` · `federation_state_list_mesh_gateways.go:33`←`:4665` · `gateway_services.go:33`←`:4666` · `health.go:45`←`health.go:40` (**unwired**, `agent.go:4670` commented out) · `health.go:63`←`health.go:74` · `health_blocking.go:46`←`:4671` · `service_http_checks.go:30`←`:4672` · `intentions.go:75`←`:4673` · `intention_upstreams.go:49`←`:4674` **and** `:4675` · `internal_service_dump.go:56`←`:4676` · `peering_list.go:33`←`:4677` · `peered_upstreams.go:32`←`:4678` · `resolved_service_config.go:34`←`:4679` · `service_list.go:31`←`:4680` · `service_list.go:48`←`service_list.go:64` · `trust_bundle.go:35`←`:4681` · `trust_bundle.go:83`←`:4682`

---

## D. Tests

### D1. Existing tests that will need updating
| File:line | Why |
|---|---|
| `agent/consul/server_test.go:274` `testServer` | Construction helper — signature changes with `NewServer` |
| `agent/consul/server_test.go:310` `testServerWithDepsAndConfig` | Same; also does `t.Cleanup(Shutdown)` |
| `agent/consul/server_test.go:417` `newServerWithDeps` | Same |
| `agent/consul/server_test.go:1190` `TestServer_LeaveLeader`, `:1240` `TestServer_Leave` | `Leave` (`server.go:1330`) interacts with the new join |
| `agent/consul/server_test.go:1551` `TestServer_Expect`, `:1644` `TestServer_Expect_NonVoters` | Multi-server construction |
| `agent/consul/server_test.go:1295` `TestServer_RPC_MetricsIntercept_Off`, `:1397` `TestServer_RPC_RequestRecorder`, `:2098` `TestServer_RPC_RateLimit` | Exercise `setupRPC` / limiter wiring |
| `agent/consul/rate/handler_test.go:24` `TestHandler`, `:436` `TestAllow` | `Register` gains a mutex |
| `agent/agent_test.go:182` `TestAgent_StartStop` | The nearest thing to a lifecycle test |
| `agent/agent_test.go:4188`, `:4230`, `:4274`, `:4319`, `:4353`, `:4375`, `:4428` (ReloadConfig family) | Reload is the only existing "change at runtime" path |
| `agent/consul/gateway_locator_test.go`, `peering_backend_test.go`, `servercert/manager_test.go` | Cover holders being re-pointed |
| `agent/proxycfg-glue/*_test.go` (16 files) | All construct `ServerDataSourceDeps` by hand; the struct changes |

### D2. Tests that must be **added** — the coverage holes

1. **`agent/consul/server_grpc_test.go` — does not exist.** 35 routes (§C1, and the largest single risk cluster) have **zero** direct test coverage. Add one that asserts every `register*Server` in `server_grpc.go:181-542` takes its dependency from the indirection object rather than capturing `s`.

2. **Restart leak test.** Extend `agent/routine-leak-checker/leak_test.go` — it already does exactly `setupPrimaryServer(t); primaryServer.Shutdown()` under `goleak.VerifyNone` at `:115-122`. Add a start→shutdown→start→shutdown cycle. This is the single highest-value new test: it catches the missing WaitGroup join, the four un-shutdown error paths (`server.go:791`, `:796`, `:819`, `:871`), and the unstopped `routineManager` (`:435`).

3. **Conn-pool survival test.** Assert `flat.ConnPool` (`agent/setup.go:165`) is still usable after `Server.Shutdown()` — pins the `server.go:1293` fix.

4. **Gateway-resolver re-point test.** After replacement, assert `connPool.GatewayResolver` (`server.go:579`) and the grpc pool's resolver (`:580`) no longer reference the old locator.

5. **`rate.Handler` concurrent re-register test** — `Register` (`handler.go:337`) racing `Allow` (`handler.go:310`) under `-race`.

6. **servercert channel test** — assert the CertManager started at `agent/agent.go:723` stops when the *server* stops, not only when the agent does.

7. **Real-wiring tests to defeat the mocks.** 17 interfaces on these routes have generated mocks, so the current suite passes regardless of production wiring: `agent/consul/rate/handler.go:153` · `agent/consul/reporting/reporting.go:52` · `connectca/server.go:46`, `:51` · `serverdiscovery/server.go:31` · `dataplane/server.go:36` · `peerstream/server.go:53` · `acl/server.go:33`, `:38`, `:43` · `resource/server.go:30`, `:35`, `:40`, `:45` · `agent/submatview/local_materializer.go:42` · `v1compat/controller.go:30` · `agent/connect/ca/provider.go:11`. Each needs at least one test using the **real** satisfier.

8. **proxycfg-glue fan-out test** — assert all 20 copies (§C2) observe a replaced server; today nothing links them back to `agent/agent.go:4653`.

9. **peerstream stream-teardown test** — long-lived stream (`stream_resources.go:345`) plus per-service `Subscriber` (`subscription_manager.go:750`) surviving a replacement.

`sense_blast(consul.Server)` reported `affected_tests: []` / `tests_affected_count: 0` — no existing test targets server construction/teardown as a unit. Items 1 and 2 close that.

---

## E. What a reviewer should check on the diff

1. `agent/consul/server.go:1293` gone or moved.
2. All four of `server.go:791`, `:796`, `:819`, `:871` call `Shutdown()`.
3. `routineManager` (`:435`) stopped alongside `leaderRoutineManager` (`:1255`).
4. `Shutdown` joins before returning; every `go` at `:531`, `:570`, `:670`, `:728`, `:752`, `:781`, `:784`, `:787`, `:788`, `:822`, `:824`, `:832`, `:848`, `:852`, `:856`, `:860` accounted for.
5. `rate/handler.go:337` synchronised; the double register (`server.go:525` + `agent.go:710`) resolved to one.
6. `agent/agent.go:723` uses the server's lifetime, not `a.shutdownCh`.
7. Zero remaining closures capturing `s` in `server_grpc.go:181-542` — grep the diff for `func(` inside `register*Server`.
8. `agent/agent.go:4652` `if server != nil` no longer a one-shot snapshot.
9. `server.go:579-580` back-edges re-pointed.
10. `rpc.go:128` reads `s.shutdown` under the lock.

---

## F. Landing order

**Phase 1 — bug fixes, no behaviour change, ships alone.** `server.go:1293`; the four error returns `:791`/`:796`/`:819`/`:871`; `routineManager` stop; `rpc.go:128` race; mutex on `rate/handler.go:337`. *Test:* restart leak test (D2.2), conn-pool survival (D2.3).

**Phase 2 — join before return.** WaitGroup across all 16 goroutine starts; `Shutdown` joins. *Test:* D2.2 extended to N cycles.

**Phase 3 — introduce the indirection object.** New type + agent-side ownership; convert `agent/agent.go:249`, `:709`, and `agent/setup.go:77`. No holders converted yet. *Test:* D2.5.

**Phase 4 — convert holders, one area per PR.** Order by risk: (a) `server_grpc.go:181-542` §C1 + `server_grpc_test.go` (D2.1); (b) `agent/agent.go:4653-4682` §C2 + D2.8; (c) peerstream §C3 + D2.9; (d) `*ACLResolver`/publisher §C4-C5; (e) wrapper carriers §C6; (f) bare-`*Server` interfaces §C7; (g) `internal/` §C8; (h) typed fields §C9 (mechanical — 26 of 32 are RPC endpoints rebuilt by the single loop at `server.go:1176`).

**Phase 5 — enable replacement.** Only after phases 1-4. Add the replace path in `agent/agent.go` near `:704`/`:1713`; re-point every seam; run the full restart leak suite.

**Do not skip to Phase 5.** Replacement on today's `Shutdown` — which returns before goroutines drain, destroys the agent's conn pool, and leaves 74 handles with no way to be re-pointed — will produce two live servers sharing a raft data directory.

Everything below is verified against the tree at `HEAD` (`2f37d52`). 106 retaining fields, 74 with no compile-time tie. Land in the 5 phases in §F.

## A. The seam to introduce

The codebase already contains the right pattern, in production, working: **`LazyNetRPC`** at `agent/setup.go:75-95` — a mutex-guarded indirection, injected once at `agent/agent.go:531`, re-pointable via `SetNetRPC` (`agent/setup.go:80-84`), with a not-ready error at `:92`.

Generalise it. Introduce one indirection object (`ServerHandle`) created by the **agent**, injected into every subsystem in place of `*Server` / narrowed views / closures over `s`, and re-pointed on replacement. Without it, 104 of 106 routes have nowhere to write the new pointer.

Two properties are non-negotiable, both learned from failures in the current code:
- **Mutex-guarded** — `rate.Handler.Register` (`agent/consul/rate/handler.go:337-339`) is the counter-example: an unsynchronised write read on the hot path at `handler.go:310`.
- **Explicit not-ready state** — `NewServer` distributes `s` from `agent/consul/server.go:525` onward while `s.fsm`, `s.raft`, `s.ACLResolver` are all still nil (created at `:535`, `:659`, `:640`).

## F. Landing order

**Phase 1 — bug fixes, no behaviour change, ships alone.** `server.go:1293`; the four error returns `:791`/`:796`/`:819`/`:871`; `routineManager` stop; `rpc.go:128` race; mutex on `rate/handler.go:337`. *Test:* restart leak test (D2.2), conn-pool survival (D2.3).

**Phase 2 — join before return.** WaitGroup across all 16 goroutine starts; `Shutdown` joins. *Test:* D2.2 extended to N cycles.

**Phase 3 — introduce the indirection object.** New type + agent-side ownership; convert `agent/agent.go:249`, `:709`, and `agent/setup.go:77`. No holders converted yet. *Test:* D2.5.

**Phase 4 — convert holders, one area per PR.** Order by risk: (a) `server_grpc.go:181-542` §C1 + `server_grpc_test.go` (D2.1); (b) `agent/agent.go:4653-4682` §C2 + D2.8; (c) peerstream §C3 + D2.9; (d) `*ACLResolver`/publisher §C4-C5; (e) wrapper carriers §C6; (f) bare-`*Server` interfaces §C7; (g) `internal/` §C8; (h) typed fields §C9 (mechanical — 26 of 32 are RPC endpoints rebuilt by the single loop at `server.go:1176`).

**Phase 5 — enable replacement.** Only after phases 1-4. Add the replace path in `agent/agent.go` near `:704`/`:1713`; re-point every seam; run the full restart leak suite.

**Do not skip to Phase 5.** Replacement on today's `Shutdown` — which returns before goroutines drain, destroys the agent's conn pool, and leaves 74 handles with no way to be re-pointed — will produce two live servers sharing a raft data directory.
